name: ai-config
goal: Establish unified AI configuration for Areté: create AIService wrapper around pi-ai, migrate backend from pi-coding-agent to direct pi-ai calls, add CLI credential management, onboarding integration, and config commands. Enables multi-model task routing and unblocks Intelligence Tuning.

userStories.title: Core AIService Foundation
userStories.description: Create AIService in @arete/core that wraps pi-ai with task-based model routing, credential loading, and structured output support. Add ai section to AreteConfig, create credentials module for secure API key storage.
acceptanceCriteria: AIService can call pi-ai with task-based model routing (task → tier → model)
callStructured() returns typed JSON responses using TypeBox schema
Credentials loaded from ~/.arete/credentials.yaml with 600 permissions
Env var override works (env > file) - existing env vars not overwritten
Graceful error when no API key configured (descriptive error message)
dev/catalog/capabilities.json updated with ai-service entry
Tests with mocked pi-ai calls (no real API calls in tests)

userStories.title: Backend Migration to pi-ai
userStories.description: Replace pi-coding-agent with pi-ai direct calls in backend. Rewrite runProcessingSession() to use AIService for meeting processing (summarization, extraction). Remove agent session overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: runProcessingSession() uses AIService instead of pi-coding-agent
Meeting processing produces same output format (staged sections with action items, decisions, learnings)
pi-coding-agent removed from backend package.json
Existing tests pass with mocked AIService
Error handling for no API key (descriptive error, job marked as error)

userStories.title: CLI Credential Management
userStories.description: Add CLI commands to manage API keys: arete credentials set <provider>, arete credentials show, arete credentials test. Store credentials securely in ~/.arete/credentials.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: arete credentials set anthropic prompts for key, validates with test call, saves securely
arete credentials show lists providers without exposing keys (masked output)
arete credentials test verifies each configured provider with test call
File created with 0600 permissions (owner read/write only)
Help text explains env var override behavior

userStories.title: Onboarding Integration
userStories.description: Add AI configuration step to arete onboard. After profile setup, prompt for Anthropic API key (skippable). Validate key before saving, write default tier config to arete.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: arete onboard prompts for Anthropic API key after profile setup
User can skip (Enter) — onboarding continues without AI config
API key validated before saving (test call to provider)
Default tier config written to arete.yaml when key provided
Re-running onboard allows updating existing key (idempotent)

userStories.title: Config CLI Commands
userStories.description: Add CLI commands to view and modify AI settings: arete config show ai, arete config set ai.tiers.<tier> <model>, arete config set ai.tasks.<task> <tier>. Validate model and tier names.
acceptanceCriteria: arete config show ai displays full AI config (tiers, tasks, configured providers)
arete config set ai.tiers.fast <model> modifies arete.yaml
arete config set ai.tasks.summary <tier> modifies arete.yaml
Invalid model names show warning but allow (pi-ai model list check)
Invalid tier names rejected with helpful error
Changes take effect on next command (config reloaded)
